revision.text:
[[ja:皇嗣]][[th:โคชิ (มกุฎราชกุมาร)]] '''Kōshi''' (皇嗣, Crown Prince) refers to a term in the {{ill|Imperial Household Law|en|Imperial Household Law|qid=Q1324733|lt=Imperial House Law|1=Imperial Household Law}} for a member of the {{ill|Imperial Family of Japan|ja|皇族|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Family of Japan|1=Imperial Family of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11581068}} who holds the first position in the {{ill|Imperial Succession|ja|皇位継承|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Succession|1=The succession to the throne|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11580995}} Ranking.{{Cite news|title=立皇嗣の礼は20th year 4月19日に　19th year のGWは10連休: 日本経済新聞|date=2018-10-12|url=https://www.nikkei.com/article/DGXMZO36403670S8A011C1MM0000/|accessdate=2018-10-12|language=ja-JP|work=日本経済新聞}} Under the current Imperial House Law, the title {{ill|Crown prince|en|Crown prince|qid=Q207293|lt=Crown Prince|1=Crown prince}} (Kōtaishi) is designated for a {{ill|Crown prince|en|Crown prince|qid=Q207293|lt=Crown Prince of Japan|1=Crown prince}} who is the Emperor’s son, while the title {{ill|Crown Grandson|ja|皇太孫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Crown Grandson|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 皇太子|1=Crown prince|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q207293}} (Kōtaison) is assigned to the Emperor's grandson. During the reign of {{ill|Naruhito|en|Naruhito|qid=Q217096|lt=Emperor Naruhito|1=Naruhito}}, the Kōshi is his younger brother, {{ill|Fumihito, Crown Prince of Japan|en|Fumihito, Crown Prince of Japan|qid=Q311174|lt=Prince Akishino|1=Fumihito, Crown Prince of Japan}}, the second son of Emperor Emeritus {{ill|Akihito|en|Akihito|qid=Q37979|lt=Akihito|1=Akihito}}. Since the Imperial House Law does not provide for the title {{ill|Crown Younger Brother|ja|皇太弟|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Crown Younger Brother|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 皇太子|1=Crown prince|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q207293}} (Kōtaitei) for an Emperor’s brother, the term Kōshi is used as a title-like appellation for Prince Akishino. Additionally, {{ill|Emperor Taishō|en|Emperor Taishō|qid=Q157052|lt=Emperor Taishō|1=Emperor Taishō}} (then Prince Yoshihito) was the non-legitimate child of {{ill|Emperor Meiji|en|Emperor Meiji|qid=Q174231|lt=Emperor Meiji|1=Emperor Meiji}}. Under the former Imperial House Law, succession within the same Rank was determined by legitimacy and birth order, meaning the succession order could be lowered if a legitimate younger brother were born. However, Prince Yoshihito was designated as Crown Prince through the {{ill|Ceremony of Investiture|ja|立太子の礼|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ceremony of Investiture|1=Investiture as Crown Prince|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11597819}} when he was ten years old and Empress Dowager Shōken (Emperor Meiji’s consort) was 40. == Overview == === Japan === {{main|Crown Prince of Japan#Japan}} In ancient Japan, succession to the position of {{ill|Ōkimi|ja|大王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkimi|1=大王|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11437799}} was unstable. Prominent candidates for succession were given the title {{ill|Ōe|ja|大兄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōe|1=ooe|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11432459}} (Great Elder Brother), but the title was not exclusive to one individual, leading to frequent conflicts within the Imperial Family over succession. During the {{ill|Ritsuryō|en|Ritsuryō|qid=Q742460|lt=Ritsuryō system|1=Ritsuryō}}, the {{ill|Succession to the Japanese throne|en|Succession to the Japanese throne|qid=Q1207573|lt=Order of Imperial Succession|1=Succession to the Japanese throne}} was not explicitly codified, resulting in power struggles between the Emperor’s sons (Crown Princes) and brothers (Crown Younger Brothers). To prevent such conflicts, Emperors often formalized succession through the investiture of Crown Princes, Crown Grandsons, or Crown Younger Brothers during their reign or specified their successor in an abdication decree to ensure stability. During the periods of {{ill|Regency Politics|ja|摂関政治|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Regency Politics|1=Sekkan Seiji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q588543}} and {{ill|Cloistered rule|en|Cloistered rule|qid=Q865416|lt=Insei|1=Cloistered rule}}, succession was influenced by the will of the Emperor’s maternal relatives (such as the Fujiwara clan) or the ruling {{ill|Cloistered Emperor|ja|治天の君|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cloistered Emperor|1=Chiten no kimi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11554403}}. === Under the Former Imperial House Law === {{kyujitai|'''Former Imperial House Law (1889):'''
'''Article 9:''' If the '''Kōshi''' has a mental or physical incurable condition or a serious issue, the Imperial Council may deliberate and alter the order of succession based on preceding articles.
'''Article 10:''' When the Emperor passes away, the '''Kōshi''' shall immediately ascend the throne and inherit the sacred regalia of the ancestors.}} The first instance of a non-Crown Prince serving as Kōshi under the modern constitutional system occurred when {{ill|Yasuhito, Prince Chichibu|en|Yasuhito, Prince Chichibu|qid=Q700317|lt=Prince Chichibu|1=Yasuhito, Prince Chichibu}}, younger brother of Emperor Shōwa, was designated Kōshi. However, he lost this status upon the birth of {{ill|Akihito|en|Akihito|qid=Q37979|lt=Akihito|1=Akihito}}, later Emperor Emeritus, in 1933.2020th year 11月10日中日新聞朝刊29面 === Under the Current System === The {{ill|Imperial Household Law|en|Imperial Household Law|qid=Q1324733|lt=Imperial House Law (1947)|1=Imperial Household Law}} specifies the following:
;Article 3: If the '''Kōshi''' has an incurable mental or physical illness or a serious issue, the order of succession may be altered according to the preceding article by the decision of the Imperial Council. ;Article 4: When the Emperor passes away, the '''Kōshi''' shall immediately ascend the throne. ;Article 8: The '''Kōshi''' who is an imperial prince is called the {{ill|Crown prince|en|Crown prince|qid=Q207293|lt=Crown Prince|1=Crown prince}}. If there is no Crown Prince, the '''Kōshi''' who is an imperial grandson is called the {{ill|Crown Grandson|ja|皇太孫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Crown Grandson|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 皇太子|1=Crown prince|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q207293}}.
Under the current law, the Kōshi cannot relinquish their Imperial status. They receive triple the Imperial allowance, and the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency Kōshi Office|ja|宮内庁皇嗣職|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Household Agency Kōshi Office|1=Crown Prince's Household|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q85879449}} manages their affairs. Their outings are referred to as “Onari.” == Kōshi (Prince Akishino) == [[File:Prince Fumihito Brazil 2015 (1).jpg|thumb|200px|Prince Akishino, the current Kōshi]] [[File:Flag of the Japanese Crown Prince.svg|thumb|200px|The flag of the Kōshi used by Prince Akishino after the investiture ceremony]] Under the {{ill|Emperor Abdication Law|en|Emperor Abdication Law|qid=Q30935738|lt=Special Measures Law on the Emperor's Abdication|1=Emperor Abdication Law}}, promulgated in June 2017, Emperor Emeritus Akihito abdicated on April 30, 2019, and Emperor Naruhito ascended to the throne on May 1, 2019. Consequently, Prince Akishino became Kōshi (the first in the line of succession).{{Cite news|url=https://mainichi.jp/articles/20171208/k00/00e/010/194000c |title=「退位19th year 4月30日」決定 新元号、来年公表|date=2017-12-08|publisher=毎日新聞|accessdate=2017-12-08}} The title "Kōshi" is used for Prince Akishino, and he is formally addressed as “His Imperial Highness Prince Akishino, Kōshi.”{{Cite web|和書|title=最終報告 |format=PDF|url=https://www.kantei.go.jp/jp/singi/koumu_keigen/pdf/saisyuhoukoku.pdf|author=天皇の公務の負担軽減等に関する有識者会議|publisher=首相官邸|date=2017-04-21|accessdate=2017-05-23}} The {{ill|Investiture Ceremony of the Kōshi|ja|立皇嗣の礼|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Investiture Ceremony of the Kōshi|1=Ceremony for Proclamation of Crown Prince|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q63346225}} was conducted on November 8, 2020, to proclaim Prince Akishino’s position as Kōshi.{{Cite news|url=https://www3.nhk.or.jp/news/html/20200414/k10012385841000.html|title=「立皇嗣の礼」延期を決定 新型コロナ感染拡大で|work=NHK NEWSWEB|agency=[[日本放送協会|NHK]]|date=2020-04-14|accessdate=2020-08-14}} == Notes == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Crown prince|en|Crown prince|qid=Q207293|lt=Crown Prince|1=Crown prince}} * {{ill|Chrysanthemum Throne|en|Chrysanthemum Throne|qid=Q1981690|lt=Imperial Succession|1=Chrysanthemum Throne}} * {{ill|Succession to the Japanese throne|en|Succession to the Japanese throne|qid=Q1207573|lt=Order of Imperial Succession|1=Succession to the Japanese throne}} * {{ill|Investiture Ceremony of the Kōshi|ja|立皇嗣の礼|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Investiture Ceremony of the Kōshi|1=Ceremony for Proclamation of Crown Prince|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q63346225}} * {{ill|Imperial Household Agency Kōshi Office|ja|宮内庁皇嗣職|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Household Agency Kōshi Office|1=Crown Prince's Household|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q85879449}} * {{ill|Imperial Allowance|ja|皇族費|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Allowance|1=kouzokuhi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q28692694}} * {{ill|Onari|ja|御成り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onari|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 御成|1=Onari|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q28691622}} {{Poli-stub}} {{translated page|ja|皇嗣|version=103323414|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:皇嗣]] [[th:โคชิ (มกุฎราชกุมาร)]] {{wikidata link|Q11581005}} [[Category:Crown Princes of Japan]] [[Category:Heirs apparent]] [[Category:Imperial transitions of Japan]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]
